news.summary: Atlanta-based Guardian Pharmacy Services, Inc. (NYSE: GRDN) has disclosed the pricing details for its underwritten public offering. The offering involves 7,500,000 shares of its Class A common stock, priced at $21.00 per share. Of these shares, 6,059,553 will be sold by specific selling stockholders, while 1,440,447 shares will be issued and sold by Guardian as part of a non-dilutive "synthetic secondary" transaction. This announcement comes in the midst of market fluctuation and aims to raise substantial capital for the company's future endeavors.
news.headline: Guardian Pharmacy Services prices underwritten public offering of 7.5M shares of Class A stock at $21 per share.

news.summary: In San Jose, California on May 22, 2025, Chenbro, a leading company in server chassis manufacturing with a TWSE listing under 8210, is showcasing its largest presence yet at COMPUTEX 2025 in Taipei from May 20 to 23. news.headline: Chenbro showcasing AI server chassis solutions at COMPUTEX 2025 in Taipei; making significant appearance. #TWSE: 8210

news.summary: Leading cryptocurrencies saw significant gains on Thursday, with Bitcoin rising by 1.30% to $111,396.80, Ethereum surging by 3.81% to $2,686.39, and Dogecoin climbing by 2.67% to $0.2474 at 9:30 p.m. ET. Bitcoin almost hit $112,000, part of a record rally, while Ethereum increased by 4% raising its market dominance to 9.3%. Bitcoin's monthly gains were over 18%, while Ethereum saw a rise of over 47% in May. Over $300 million was liquidated from the cryptocurrency market in the last 24 hours, with shorts receiving the most impact. Bitcoin's open interest remained stable, while Ethereum experienced a 9% jump. The news attributed the sluggishness in stocks to President Donald Trump's tax bill-induced debt fears. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Bitcoin flirts with $112,000, Ethereum and Dogecoin spike. Cryptocurrency gains: Bitcoin +1.30% $111,396.80, Ethereum +3.81% $2,686.39, Dogecoin +2.67% $0.2474. Stocks flat amid debt concerns. Bitcoin up 18% in May, Ethereum up 47% month-to-date. Over $300 million liquidated in last 24 hours, with shorts hardest hit. Ethereum sees 9% jump in funds.

news.headline: Volvo Trucks Driver Challenge promotes productivity and sustainability in transport; Mr. Fatorrahman from Indonesia wins Off-Road Category.

news.summary: Outgoing CFTC Commissioner Summer Mersinger stated that regulatory approval for crypto perpetual futures contracts in the US could come "very soon." These contracts allow traders to speculate on cryptocurrency prices without owning them and can be held indefinitely, with high leverage trading. Currently not allowed in the US, they are mainly traded on offshore exchanges such as Binance, OKX, and Bybit. Binance, the largest among them, handles almost $95 billion in daily perpetual trading volume across over 500 pairs with leverage up to 125x. Mersinger emphasized the importance of having these derivatives regulated domestically, which she believed would be beneficial for both the markets and the industry as a whole. She highlighted the recent procedural vote for the GENIUS stablecoin bill as a positive sign for the industry's future in the US. Mersinger will be leaving the CFTC to join the Blockchain Association, advocating for the crypto industry. She praised the incoming CFTC chairman's crypto industry-friendly stance and expressed her desire to contribute more to the industry through her new role.
news.headline: CFTC's Mersinger anticipates approval for US crypto perpetual futures soon. Binance tops with $95B daily volume. Source: YouTube.

news.summary: A first-in-human clinical study is set to commence this year in Shanghai and San Diego. Duality Biotherapeutics, listed as HKEX:09606, will be collaborating with Avenzo Therapeutics, Inc., a clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on developing advanced oncology treatments. The study, set to be conducted in phases 1 and 2, marks a significant milestone for the companies involved in this partnership.
news.headline: FDA clears IND for novel EGFR/HER3 bispecific ADC AVZO-1418/DB-1418. Phase 1/2 study to start in 2025, by Duality Biotherapeutics partner Avenzo.

news.summary: Trung Nguyen, operating an unlicensed money-transmitting business named National Vending from September 2017 to October 2020, was sentenced to six years in prison by a Boston federal court. He was ordered to forfeit $1.5 million. Nguyen converted over $1 million to Bitcoin without registering with FinCEN as per Anti-Money Laundering regulations. His methods involved converting cash into Bitcoin for scam victims and a drug dealer. Nguyen avoided detection by using a fake vending machine business as a front. He was caught after meeting undercover cops in person to exchange cash for Bitcoin, taking a 5% commission. Nguyen was charged with operating an unlicensed money transmitting business and two counts of money laundering, convicted on one money laundering charge, pleading not guilty to all charges.
news.headline: Bitcoin launderer sentenced to 6 years in prison, must forfeit $1.5M. Ran unlicensed business under fake vending cover. Source: Pacer.

news.summary: The U.S. Supreme Court made a significant ruling strengthening presidential control over independent federal agencies, except for the Federal Reserve. In a decision issued on Thursday, the court granted a stay allowing President Donald Trump to dismiss members of the National Labor Relations Board and the Merit Systems Protection Board without justification. However, the Federal Reserve was excluded from this ruling. The Court specified that the debate over the constitutionality of removal protections without cause does not extend to the Federal Reserve, considering it a distinctively structured, quasi-private institution with roots in the heritage of the First and Second Banks. For further details, please refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Supreme Court limits Trump's authority to fire independent agency members, except for Federal Reserve due to its unique structure.

news.headline: NVIDIA hits record 1,000 TPS/user using Llama 4 Maverick & Blackwell GPUs, setting new AI model performance standard.

news.summary: A recent report indicates that the unemployment rate in the United States has dropped to 4.2%, the lowest it has been in over a year. news.summary: Economist Peter Schiff criticized President Donald Trump for hosting an exclusive dinner with top holders of his Official Trump meme coin. Schiff stated that it should be deemed "illegal" for a U.S. president to accept direct payments for private dining events or sell White House tours for personal profit. The gala dinner involved the top 220 meme coin investors dining with Trump at his private club, while the top 25 holders were offered a VIP reception, White House tour, and special commemorative NFTs. Schiff emphasized that although campaign donations are acceptable, personal financial gains by the president should not be allowed. For more details, the full article can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Economist Peter Schiff criticizes Trump's dinner with meme coin holders, calls out for ethical concerns; top 220 holders to dine with Trump at his private country club.

news.summary: The self-driving cars market is expected to experience substantial growth, with projections increasing from 37,090 thousand units in 2024 to 76,217 thousand units in 2035, at a CAGR of 6.8%. Advancements in sensor technologies like LiDAR and radar, along with the adoption of vehicle-to-everything communication and the rise of mobility-as-a-service solutions, are key drivers for this growth. Factors such as government policies, consumer demand for safer mobility solutions, cloud computing, AI-based decision-making, emerging markets, and intelligent city initiatives are also contributing to the expansion of the market. Key players in this market include Toyota, Tesla, BYD, Volkswagen, Ford, and Hyundai. The personal mobility segment is predicted to dominate the market, with a surge in demand for autonomous vehicles offering convenience and on-demand transportation. In 2024, Toyota's Corolla, a Level 1 autonomous vehicle, sold over 1.2 million units globally from January to September, indicating a growing interest in ADAS-equipped vehicles.
news.headline: Self-driving cars market to reach 76,217 thousand units in 2035 at 6.8% CAGR, driven by sensor tech advancements and consumer demand.

news.summary: In a bold move to drive market innovation and enhance educational outcomes, Think Together has introduced a new C-suite position. The California-based nonprofit organization is ramping up its services and reach across the state, solidifying its position as a key player in the education sector. The announcement was made on May 22, 2025 in Santa Ana, California, marking a significant step in Think Together's expansion efforts. With this development, Think Together aims to make a larger impact on California's education landscape.
news.headline: Think Together appoints Matt Scharpnick as Chief of Innovation to drive market growth and education outcomes in California.

news.summary: Michigan lawmakers have introduced four crypto-related bills, including House Bill 4510 allowing investment in cryptocurrencies with a market cap above $250 million like Bitcoin and Ether. Another bill allows up to 10% of Michigan’s Budget Stabilization Fund to be allocated into crypto. A bipartisan bill, HB 4511, aims to prevent Michigan from banning crypto or imposing licensing requirements on holders and prohibits supporting a proposed central bank digital currency (CBDC). Furthermore, HB 4512 proposes a Bitcoin mining program at abandoned oil and gas sites, while HB 4513 seeks to amend income tax laws to include income from Bitcoin mining.
news.headline: Michigan lawmakers introduce 4 crypto bills: mining, CBDCs, retirement funds. Bills cover investments over $250M, banning crypto bans.
